key_findings: Travel lodging is governed by 04_travel_expense.md Article 4, using a two-dimensional matrix: employee level (员工级/经理级/部门负责人级/高管级, 4 levels) × city tier (一类/二类/三类, 3 tiers), producing 12 distinct standards ranging from 300 to 1,100 元/人/晚.

Training lodging is governed by 05_training_expense.md Article 5, using a single dimension: city tier only (一类/二类/三类), producing 3 flat standards: 500/420/350 元/人/晚, with no employee level differentiation.

Both policies contain explicit mutual-exclusion clauses: 04_travel_expense.md Article 7 states training-period lodging must use the training policy; 05_training_expense.md Article 6 states training lodging standards must not be mixed with travel lodging standards.

The general reimbursement policy (01_expense_reimbursement_2025.md) Article 12 caps all reimbursement at 1.0× the applicable standard absent special approval, applicable to both travel and training lodging.

Numerical comparison for Tier 1 cities (一类城市): training lodging = 500元 vs travel lodging 员工级 = 450元 (training +50元), 经理级 = 650元 (travel +150元) — training standard sits between staff and manager levels in all three tiers.

The two policies cannot be mixed because they differ in: (a) triggering scope — business travel vs training activity; (b) standard dimensions — employee level matters for travel but not training; (c) numerical values — training standards (500/420/350) do not correspond to any single employee level in the travel table; (d) explicit bilateral prohibitions in both policies.
